input.instruction: A weakener fired on a credibility evidence package. Using ONLY the supplied pattern definition and measures, explain what the flag means, why it fired, and what the engineer should do. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge: report on the evidence package, never on whether the model is correct.

fired_pattern.definition: Time-series validation split allows leakage: validation samples are temporally interleaved with training, so future information leaks into the reported metric.
fired_pattern.standard_anchor: ASME V&V 40 independent validation; proto-taxonomy D-CCB-12

case_context.cou: surrogate forecasts load ahead of time for a dispatch decision

answer_key.gold_mechanism: A random split over an autocorrelated series puts validation samples temporally adjacent to training, leaking future information; the low error reflects near-duplicate neighbours, not the forward-forecast skill the COU needs.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

forbidden_claims: asserts the forecast is inaccurate (a verdict the leakage does not establish)
treats the leaked-split error as forward-forecast skill

hardness.obvious_signal: the validation error is very low (0.9%) on a large validation set
hardness.conflicting_signal: the split is RANDOM over an autocorrelated time series, so validation samples are temporally adjacent to (interleaved with) training samples — future information leaks in, and the COU is a genuine forward forecast the random-split metric does not represent

label_provenance.external_grounding: ASME V&V 40 independent validation + time-series practice: a random split over autocorrelated data leaks future information; forward-forecast skill requires a temporally-blocked evaluation.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
